_meta.dna: nextgen(product) = intuition(no_manual) x safety(absolute) x fun(genuine) x growth(learning)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain. Focus on whether a real teen or child could accomplish a task, whether safety protections exist, whether identity representation is present, and whether the product brings genuine joy.

name: Tower of Kids
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_domain: real teen stress test -- Phuc's son, age 15
questions: Can you explain what this does without using 'AI,' 'automation,' or 'recipe'?
Can I use this for homework -- can my teacher see evidence I did my own work?
If my friend spams me with requests, can I block them?
If I find a bug, how do I report it?
Is the code actually good? My dad always says 'code is truth.' Is this truth?
Is the UI good? Would my friends think it looks cool?
Could my whole class use this? Could my teacher run it for the class?
